mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-to-medium horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth consistently fails to correlate positively with SPX 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a specific threshold, it signals potential future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. This typically dampens equity valuations by increasing discount rates and reducing corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the trigger threshold, or if FEDFUNDS does not rise within 6 months of the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates strong consumer demand and increasing purchasing power. This directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 upward over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The combination of strong retail sales growth and falling unemployment fails to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of exceptionally low interest rates (FEDFUNDS near zero), strong housing starts indicate a recovering economy and increased investment. This is highly supportive of equity markets as it signals broader economic health and future growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, HOUST exceeding 800.0 (Thousands of Units) does not lead to positive SPX 60-day returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing activity and business investment. This signals a robust economic environment that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO 1-month growth consistently shows a Rank IC below 0.02 with SPX 5-day returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction or recession. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, thus negatively impacting S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment as a lagging recession indicator
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is very low, indicating accommodative monetary policy, and GDP growth is strong, it creates an ideal environment for equity markets. This is due to cheap capital and robust economic activity, driving S&P 500 returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of low FEDFUNDS and strong GDPC1 growth fails to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, strong retail sales may indicate overheating demand, prompting central bank tightening. This leads to negative equity returns as discount rates rise and future growth prospects dim due to policy intervention.
evidence_refs: fact:historical inflation spikes and market reactions
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is above 4%, strong RSAFS growth does not lead to negative SPX 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases.
